subject: exfat: fix memory mapped file ops.
author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: No effect on 6.18 builds, which use vanilla exfat. In 5.15 stock builds with Samsung driver, memory-mapped file operations would not have been optimized (version ifdefs removed, mmu_private validation added, exfat_direct_IO simplified).

notes: Part of 6-commit Samsung exfat driver replacement (patch-provenance.md Class G, row 3.8). Refactors exfat_super.c memory-mapped file operations by: (1) removing 8 kernel-version ifdefs (#if KERNEL_VERSION < 2.6.34/2.6.39/3.4/3.12/3.16/4.1/4.2/4.7); (2) simplifying exfat_readpage/writepage/writepages via direct returns; (3) adding mmu_private validation in exfat_write_end(); (4) refactoring exfat_direct_IO() logic with detailed comments; (5) adding .set_page_dirty=__set_page_dirty_buffers to address_space_operations; (6) renaming _exfat_bmap→exfat_aop_bmap. Samsung driver uses different APIs than vanilla 6.18 exfat (e.g., direct_IO signature, readpage vs. folio API, no valid_size tracking). Per ADR 0019 (2026-07-14), 6.18 build uses vanilla exfat with symlink support via 0031-exfat-samsung-symlinks.patch. Vanilla 6.18 implements equivalent memory-mapped file functionality via different code paths. This commit is neither carried to 6.18 nor appears in patch-provenance.md entries (0001–0031); instead handled as part of the Samsung driver block drop at P0.9. upstream.equivalence corrected from 'contradicted' to null: the field had no vanilla_file_line/vanilla_quote backing it, and the notes' own claim ('vanilla 6.18 implements equivalent memory-mapped file functionality via different code paths') is an 'equivalent' characterization, not 'contradicted' — the two were self-inconsistent. Since this is a dropped-deliberate commit modifying a driver (Samsung exfat) that is not part of our 6.18 build architecture at all, no upstream comparison is actually being asserted; equivalence=null is correct per the same reasoning applied to sibling record 8b6b8c2f5. sonnet-verified: disposition=dropped-deliberate and severity=none confirmed correct; equivalence-flag semantics fixed.
